sources.issuing_organisation: American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons
sources.exact_document_title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Anal Fissures
sources.jurisdiction: United States; international colorectal evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Adults with acute or chronic anal fissure; paediatric, pregnancy, inflammatory, infectious, malignant, and immunocompromised contexts require separate evidence.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, colorectal, and surgical assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Supports symptom pattern, duration, stool context, bleeding, atypical-location context, and clinician-performed anorectal assessment without diagnosing fissure or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Statement of the problem — presentation and symptom pattern
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.

exact_sections.heading: Atypical fissure context and chronicity findings
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-observed location, multiplicity, chronicity features, and context requiring broader assessment without asserting a cause.

sources.issuing_organisation: American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons
sources.exact_document_title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids
sources.jurisdiction: United States; international colorectal evidence requiring UAE adaptation
sources.population: Adults with haemorrhoidal symptoms; other causes of anorectal bleeding, pain, discharge, or mass require separate assessment.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, gastroenterology, colorectal, and surgical assessment or follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Supports bowel-habit, bleeding, prolapse, discomfort, hygiene, prior treatment, and clinician-performed examination documentation without diagnosing haemorrhoids or recommending intervention.

exact_sections.heading: Evaluation and diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.

exact_sections.heading: Office, operative, and follow-up considerations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people with established Crohn’s disease.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
sources.applicability_note: Supports symptom, relapse, treatment-monitoring, perianal, nutrition, prior surgery, patient-view, and follow-up documentation without assigning activity or generating treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.11 — information, assessment, and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports age-appropriate information, multidisciplinary discussion, symptoms, nutritional status, growth, treatment context, and safety-monitoring records.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.3 — remission and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording patient views, relapse symptoms, agreed follow-up, access arrangements, and surveillance status.
